OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

That's why I think it's crucial to manage our social media use and be more cautious when sharing or consuming information online. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a hairdresser living in Rome, I've always believed that it's essential to be aware of the information we consume on social media. With the rise of fake content, it's become increasingly challenging to distinguish between what's real and what's not. As someone who's been struggling with depression, I've noticed how fake news can exacerbate my anxiety. That's why I think it's crucial to manage our social media use and be more cautious when sharing or consuming information online. One way to do this is by verifying the sources of the information we read or share. I've started using fact-checking websites and apps that help me identify the credibility of the sources. I've also become more mindful of the time I spend on social media, trying to limit it to specific hours of the day. By being more aware of what we're consuming and sharing, we can create a safer and more trustworthy online environment. Of course, it's not always easy, and it requires effort and discipline. But I believe it's worth it, especially considering the impact it can have on our mental health and well-being. For instance, I've noticed that when I'm not exposed to fake news, I feel more calm and confident. It's amazing how much of a difference it can make. I've even started to share articles and posts that promote critical thinking and media literacy, hoping to inspire others to do the same. We need to be responsible digital citizens and take control of the information we consume. By doing so, we can create a more positive and trustworthy online community.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I realized that fake content on social media was affecting my mental health, so I started taking steps to manage my social media use and verify the sources of the information I consume. I use fact-checking websites and apps to identify credible sources and limit my social media use to specific hours of the day. I also try to share articles and posts that promote critical thinking and media literacy, hoping to inspire others to do the same. I believe it's essential to be aware of the information we consume and share online, and to take control of our digital presence.
